channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: EU Delegation to China Funded Internship 2026 in Beijing: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Graduates Interested in Diplomacy, International Relations, Communications, and Translation.
item.dc:creator: Hildah W. Nakafu
item.description:
The European Union Delegation to China has announced an exciting funded internship opportunity for young graduates who are passionate about international relations, diplomacy, communications, media, translation, and EU-China cooperation. The internship program will take place in Beijing, China, and is expected to begin in October 2026. This prestigious internship offers selected candidates the chance to […]

The post EU Delegation to China Funded Internship 2026 in Beijing: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Graduates Interested in Diplomacy, International Relations, Communications, and Translation. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Union Delegation to China has announced an exciting funded internship opportunity for young graduates who are passionate about international relations, diplomacy, communications, media, translation, and EU-China cooperation. The internship program will take place in Beijing, China, and is expected to begin in October 2026.

This prestigious internship offers selected candidates the chance to work directly within the EU Delegation to China, gaining practical experience inside an international diplomatic mission while contributing to impactful projects and activities. Interns will have the opportunity to engage with political affairs, media and public diplomacy, translation work, and intercultural communication in a highly professional international environment.

The internship will last for up to six months, and selected candidates will receive a monthly grant to help cover living expenses during their stay in Beijing.

For graduates looking to build careers in diplomacy, public policy, international communications, or global affairs, this internship provides invaluable exposure and professional development opportunities.

About the EU Delegation to China Internship 2026

The EU Delegation to China represents the European Union in China and works to strengthen diplomatic, political, economic, and cultural relations between the European Union and China. Through this internship program, the Delegation seeks motivated young graduates who can support its ongoing activities while gaining first-hand experience in international diplomacy.

Interns will work closely with professionals and experts across various departments within the delegation. The experience is designed to provide practical training, mentorship, and exposure to international policy-making processes and diplomatic engagement.

The internship program is especially suitable for graduates with academic backgrounds or professional interests in:

  • International Relations
  • Diplomacy
  • Political Science
  • European Studies
  • Communications
  • Journalism
  • Media and Public Relations
  • Translation and Interpretation
  • Chinese Studies
  • Language and Linguistics

Internship Areas and Responsibilities

Selected interns may contribute to one or more of the following areas:

1. Political Affairs and EU-China Relations

Interns supporting political affairs will assist in monitoring political developments, conducting research, preparing reports, and contributing to diplomatic initiatives related to EU-China relations.

Responsibilities may include:

  • Researching political and international affairs topics
  • Drafting reports and policy briefs
  • Supporting meetings and diplomatic events
  • Monitoring developments related to EU-China cooperation
  • Assisting with communication between departments and stakeholders

2. Press, Public Information, and Social Media

Candidates interested in communications and media will support the Delegation’s public diplomacy and digital communication activities.

Tasks may include:

  • Managing and creating social media content
  • Supporting public information campaigns
  • Drafting press materials and communication content
  • Monitoring media coverage
  • Assisting with event communication and outreach

3. Translation, Interpretation, and Language Support

Interns with strong language skills may assist with translation and interpretation work to support communication between English-speaking and Chinese-speaking stakeholders.

Responsibilities may include:

  • Translating official documents and communication materials
  • Assisting during meetings and events
  • Providing interpretation support
  • Reviewing language accuracy and consistency
  • Supporting multilingual communication initiatives

Financial Benefits

One of the major highlights of this internship is that it is funded. Selected candidates will receive a monthly grant intended to help with living expenses during the internship period in Beijing.

Benefits include:

  • Monthly financial support
  • Hands-on experience in a diplomatic mission
  • International work exposure
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Practical experience in diplomacy and communications
  • Opportunity to strengthen international career prospects
  • Exposure to EU-China diplomatic relations

Internship Location

  • EU Delegation to China
  • Beijing, China

Beijing provides interns with the opportunity to experience one of the world’s most influential political and cultural capitals while working in an international environment.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants interested in the internship should meet the following requirements:

  • Hold a relevant university degree
  • Have strong English drafting and writing skills
  • Be fluent in Chinese
  • Possess excellent communication skills
  • Demonstrate interest in diplomacy, international affairs, communications, or translation
  • Hold a valid visa and legal authorization to stay in China throughout the internship period

Candidates who demonstrate professionalism, adaptability, intercultural communication abilities, and strong analytical skills may have an added advantage during the selection process.

Why You Should Apply

This internship is a valuable opportunity for young graduates seeking international professional experience. Working within the EU Delegation to China allows interns to develop practical skills, build international networks, and gain direct insight into diplomatic operations.

Some reasons why this internship stands out include:

  1. Exposure to International Diplomacy
    Interns gain practical understanding of how diplomatic missions operate and engage in international cooperation.
  2. Career Development Opportunities
    The internship strengthens professional profiles for careers in diplomacy, communications, international organizations, media, or public policy.
  3. Multicultural Experience
    Participants work in a diverse and international environment while experiencing life in Beijing.
  4. Professional Networking
    Interns interact with diplomats, policy experts, communication professionals, and international stakeholders.
  5. Skill Development
    The experience enhances research, communication, translation, analytical, and public diplomacy skills.

Application Deadline

The application deadline for the EU Delegation to China Funded Internship 2026 is:

12 June 2026 at 17:00 Beijing Time

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid last-minute delays.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply by email using the contact below:

Email: delegation-china-hr@eeas.europa.eu

Official application and information link:

EU Delegation to China Internship Information

Applicants should ensure they prepare all required documents and verify their visa and legal stay eligibility before applying.

Final Thoughts

The EU Delegation to China Funded Internship 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for ambitious graduates who aspire to build careers in international relations, diplomacy, communications, or translation. Beyond financial support, the internship offers meaningful professional exposure and the chance to contribute to real diplomatic and international cooperation efforts.

Graduates with strong English and Chinese language skills, excellent communication abilities, and a passion for international engagement are strongly encouraged to apply before the deadline.

FOR MORE INFORMATION ABOUT THIS.  

Discover more Internship opportunities click here

The post EU Delegation to China Funded Internship 2026 in Beijing: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Graduates Interested in Diplomacy, International Relations, Communications, and Translation. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Trieste Opens Applications for 15 Fully Research-Focused PhD Programmes in Italy for 2026/2027
item.dc:creator: Hildah W. Nakafu
item.description:
The University of Trieste has officially launched its highly anticipated Call for Applications for the 42nd Cycle of PhD Programmes for the 2026/2027 academic year. This prestigious doctoral recruitment initiative presents a major opportunity for graduates, researchers, and aspiring academics from around the world to pursue advanced research training in one of Europe’s most research-intensive […]

The post University of Trieste Opens Applications for 15 Fully Research-Focused PhD Programmes in Italy for 2026/2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Trieste has officially launched its highly anticipated Call for Applications for the 42nd Cycle of PhD Programmes for the 2026/2027 academic year. This prestigious doctoral recruitment initiative presents a major opportunity for graduates, researchers, and aspiring academics from around the world to pursue advanced research training in one of Europe’s most research-intensive environments.

Located in northeastern Italy, the University of Trieste is internationally recognized for academic excellence, interdisciplinary collaboration, and strong partnerships with scientific institutions across Europe. The latest call includes 15 doctoral programmes spanning science, engineering, medicine, law, humanities, artificial intelligence, environmental studies, and social sciences.

For students and professionals seeking funded PhD opportunities in Europe, this call stands out as one of the most comprehensive and competitive doctoral opportunities available in Italy this year.

Deadline for Applications

The official application deadline is:

25 May 2026 at 12:59 PM (local time)

Applicants are strongly encouraged to complete and submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical difficulties.

Why Study at the University of Trieste?

The University of Trieste continues to attract international researchers because of its strong research culture, global outlook, and strategic location within one of Europe’s leading scientific ecosystems.

Trieste is widely regarded as one of the continent’s most research-oriented cities and hosts numerous internationally respected institutions and innovation hubs, including AREA Science Park and several advanced research centers.

Pursuing a PhD at the University of Trieste offers students the opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research while benefiting from Italy’s rich academic heritage and high quality of life.

Key Benefits of the PhD Programmes

Applicants selected into the doctoral programmes may benefit from:

  1. Access to internationally competitive research environments
  2. Opportunities for fully funded doctoral scholarships
  3. Collaboration with leading researchers and institutions
  4. Strong interdisciplinary learning and research exposure
  5. Potential industry-linked research opportunities
  6. Online interview options for international applicants
  7. Access to advanced laboratories and research infrastructure
  8. European academic networking opportunities
  9. A multicultural and globally connected academic community
  10. Living and studying in one of Italy’s intellectually vibrant cities

List of Available PhD Programmes

The 42nd Cycle of PhD Programmes includes the following doctoral fields:

Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics

  • Applied Data Science and AI
  • Physics
  • Nanotechnology
  • Chemistry
  • Circular Economy
  • Civil-Environmental Engineering and Architecture
  • Industrial and Information Engineering
  • Earth Science, Fluid-Dynamics and Mathematics: Interactions and Methods

Health and Biomedical Sciences

  • Molecular Biomedicine
  • Personalized Medicine and Innovative Therapies
  • Neural and Cognitive Sciences
  • Biodiversity, Evolution and Adaptation

Humanities, Law, and Social Sciences

  • Philosophy, History and Political Studies
  • Advanced Interdisciplinary Legal Studies
  • Transcultural and Transmedia Studies: Literature, Linguistics, Interpreting and Translation

This wide range of disciplines makes the opportunity suitable for candidates from nearly every academic background.

Who Should Apply?

The University of Trieste PhD programmes are ideal for:

  • Graduates interested in pursuing academic research careers
  • Individuals passionate about innovation and scientific discovery
  • Researchers seeking interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Students aiming to study in Europe
  • Professionals looking to deepen expertise in specialized fields
  • Candidates interested in Artificial Intelligence, medicine, law, engineering, humanities, and environmental science

The call is open to both Italian and international applicants.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for admission into the doctoral programmes, applicants must hold an academic qualification that grants access to doctoral studies.

Eligible qualifications include:

  • An Italian Laurea Magistrale degree
  • An equivalent foreign university degree
  • Any academic qualification recognized for doctoral study access in the country where it was awarded

Applicants should carefully review the requirements for their selected programme, as some doctoral tracks may have additional eligibility conditions.

Selection Process

Admission into the University of Trieste PhD programmes is highly competitive and based on academic merit.

The selection process may include:

1. Academic Evaluation

Applicants’ educational background, transcripts, and qualifications will be reviewed.

2. Research Proposal Assessment

Certain programmes may require candidates to submit a detailed research proposal outlining intended research interests and objectives.

3. Interview

Shortlisted applicants may be invited for interviews conducted either:

  • In person, or
  • Via videoconference for international candidates

Each doctoral programme has specific admission procedures and evaluation criteria outlined in the official programme annexes.

Funding Opportunities Available

One of the major highlights of this doctoral call is the availability of funded positions.

Many selected candidates may receive:

  • Fully funded PhD scholarships
  • Tuition fee waivers
  • Social security coverage
  • Research funding support
  • Opportunities linked to specialized research projects
  • Potential collaborations with industry and innovation sectors

The number and structure of funded positions vary across programmes.

Required Application Documents

Depending on the chosen programme, applicants may need to submit:

  • Academic transcripts
  • Degree certificates
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Motivation letter
  • Research proposal
  • Reference letters
  • Language proficiency documents

Applicants are advised to carefully consult programme-specific requirements before applying.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The University of Trieste’s 2026/2027 doctoral call represents more than a standard academic opportunity. It offers a pathway into Europe’s growing research ecosystem, providing students with access to international collaborations, high-impact scientific work, and professional advancement opportunities.

For candidates seeking a fully immersive research experience in Italy, the University of Trieste provides a strong combination of academic excellence, interdisciplinary innovation, and global visibility.

The broad range of disciplines also ensures opportunities for students from both STEM and non-STEM backgrounds.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted online through the official University of Trieste admissions portal.

Official Application Portal

University of Trieste Admissions Portal

Official Deadline

25 May 2026 at 12:59 PM (local time)

For more information about this opportunity click here

Discover more PhD Scholarship opportunities on OFY here

The post University of Trieste Opens Applications for 15 Fully Research-Focused PhD Programmes in Italy for 2026/2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Call for Applications: SEAP Fellows 2026 — Fully Remote Human Rights Fellowship for Early-Career Professionals in Asia-Pacific
item.dc:creator: Hildah W. Nakafu
item.description:
The 2026 SEAP Fellowship Programme by Nationality for All (NFA) presents an outstanding opportunity for early-career human rights professionals across the Asia-Pacific region to contribute to one of the region’s most significant research initiatives on statelessness, nationality rights, and legal identity systems. As part of its continued work on the Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP), […]

The post Call for Applications: SEAP Fellows 2026 — Fully Remote Human Rights Fellowship for Early-Career Professionals in Asia-Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 2026 SEAP Fellowship Programme by Nationality for All (NFA) presents an outstanding opportunity for early-career human rights professionals across the Asia-Pacific region to contribute to one of the region’s most significant research initiatives on statelessness, nationality rights, and legal identity systems.

As part of its continued work on the Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP), NFA is recruiting two full-time fellows for a three-month remote fellowship running from June 15, 2026 to September 11, 2026. The programme is designed to strengthen regional research, advocacy, and knowledge production concerning statelessness and exclusion across Southeast Asia, East Asia, and Central Asia.

Applications close on May 25, 2026 (EOD Bangkok Time).

What is the SEAP Fellowship Programme?

The SEAP Fellowship Programme is a structured research fellowship aimed at supporting the development and verification of the Second Edition of the Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP).

The programme allows selected fellows to work directly with an international human rights organization while gaining hands-on experience in:

  • Human rights research
  • Factchecking methodologies
  • Citation management
  • Legal and policy analysis
  • Website content updating
  • Research documentation
  • Regional advocacy initiatives

The fellowship is entirely remote, making it accessible to qualified candidates throughout the Asia-Pacific region.

About the Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP)

The Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP) is one of the region’s most comprehensive research resources documenting issues related to:

  • Statelessness
  • Nationality laws
  • Citizenship rights
  • Legal identity systems
  • Human rights exclusion
  • Discrimination affecting vulnerable populations

SEAP serves as a major regional knowledge platform that supports researchers, policymakers, advocates, civil society actors, and human rights organizations working to address nationality and identity-related challenges across Asia-Pacific.

Over the years, the project has compiled extensive information on populations affected by statelessness and nationality discrimination throughout the region. The Second Edition of SEAP aims to further strengthen this research through updated factchecking, legal verification, and citation integration.

Fellowship Duration and Structure

The fellowship will run for three months on a full-time basis.

Fellowship Timeline

  • Start Date: June 15, 2026
  • End Date: September 11, 2026
  • Duration: 3 Months
  • Mode: Remote (Work-from-home)

Although the programme is remote, fellows may occasionally participate in in-person meetings if necessary.

Monthly Stipend

Selected fellows will receive a monthly stipend ranging between:

  • AUD 200 – AUD 400 per month

The exact amount will depend on the fellow’s location.

Key Responsibilities of SEAP Fellows

The selected fellows will support the NFA research team in updating and verifying SEAP’s Second Edition.

1. Factchecking and Additional Research

Fellows will:

  • Review existing SEAP country factsheets
  • Conduct supplementary desk research
  • Verify legal, policy, and statistical information
  • Identify outdated or inconsistent information
  • Cross-check regional human rights documentation

2. Website and Report Updates

Responsibilities include:

  • Updating SEAP website content
  • Revising research reports
  • Ensuring consistency across materials
  • Coordinating edits with the NFA team

3. Citation Integration and Verification

Fellows will also:

  • Add missing citations
  • Verify citation accuracy
  • Ensure source integrity
  • Maintain consistency in referencing standards

Detailed Fellowship Work Plan

Month 1: Orientation and Factchecking

During the first month, fellows will receive onboarding and practical training on:

  • Factchecking methodologies
  • Citation systems
  • Website updating procedures
  • Research verification techniques

Fellows will begin reviewing and factchecking assigned regional content during this stage.

Month 2: Website and Report Revisions

The second month focuses on:

  • Updating website materials
  • Revising reports based on verified findings
  • Collaborating with the NFA research team
  • Improving content consistency and accuracy

Month 3: Citation Integration and Final Verification

The final month involves:

  • Integrating citations into SEAP content
  • Conducting final quality checks
  • Verifying accuracy across reports and website entries
  • Finalizing updates for publication

Training and Mentorship

Selected fellows will receive extensive support throughout the programme, including:

  • A detailed factchecking template and research guide
  • Training on citation standards
  • Guidance on website updating systems
  • Ongoing mentorship from the NFA research team
  • Continuous feedback and support

This makes the programme highly beneficial for individuals seeking practical experience in international human rights research and documentation.

Eligibility Criteria for SEAP Fellows 2026

Applicants are expected to demonstrate strong academic, analytical, and organizational abilities.

Required Skills

Candidates should possess:

  • Strong desk research skills
  • Excellent analytical ability
  • Good writing skills
  • Attention to detail
  • Strong organizational skills
  • Effective time-management abilities
  • Ability to synthesize information clearly

Since the fellowship is fully remote, applicants must also be:

  • Self-driven
  • Proactive
  • Comfortable working independently
  • Reliable in meeting deadlines
  • Consistent in remote communication

Academic and Knowledge Background

Applicants should ideally have academic or professional exposure to:

  • Human rights
  • Law
  • Public policy
  • International relations
  • Related disciplines

Knowledge of the following areas is highly desirable:

  • Citizenship rights
  • Statelessness
  • Legal identity systems

However, prior expertise in these topics is not mandatory.

Preferred Experience

Although prior professional experience is not required, applicants with experience in the following areas may have an advantage:

  • Human rights initiatives
  • Research projects
  • Civil society organizations
  • Non-profit organizations
  • Advocacy programmes

Importantly, persons with lived experience of statelessness will receive priority consideration.

Why You Should Apply for the SEAP Fellowship Programme

The SEAP Fellowship offers more than just research experience. It provides fellows with an opportunity to directly contribute to meaningful regional advocacy work while building highly transferable professional skills.

Benefits of the Fellowship

Participants will have the opportunity to:

  1. Contribute to a major regional human rights initiative
  2. Gain expertise in nationality rights and statelessness
  3. Develop professional research and factchecking skills
  4. Learn citation management and documentation systems
  5. Work with an international human rights organization
  6. Build experience in regional advocacy and knowledge production
  7. Strengthen their professional profile in human rights research

For early-career professionals interested in international development, policy research, migration studies, citizenship rights, or legal identity systems, this fellowship provides valuable practical exposure.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following materials:

  • Updated CV
  • Cover Letter
  • One original writing sample

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications is:

May 25, 2026 (End of Day Bangkok Time)

Late submissions may not be considered.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should send all required application documents via email to:

info@nationalityforall.org

Application – SEAP Fellows 2026

For more information about the organization and fellowship programme, visit Nationality for All (NFA) Official Website.

The SEAP Fellowship Programme 2026 represents a valuable opportunity for emerging human rights professionals across Asia-Pacific to gain meaningful research experience while contributing to an important regional initiative focused on statelessness, nationality rights, and legal identity systems.

The fully remote structure, practical training, mentorship opportunities, and direct involvement in a major research platform make this fellowship especially attractive for individuals seeking to build careers in human rights, policy research, advocacy, and international development.

For more information about this click here.

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY here

The post Call for Applications: SEAP Fellows 2026 — Fully Remote Human Rights Fellowship for Early-Career Professionals in Asia-Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: JUST Fellowship 2026 in India: Fully Funded Field-Based Fellowship on Just and Sustainable Transitions
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Centre for Financial Accountability (CFA), in collaboration with Samata, has officially launched applications for the Just and Sustainable Transitions (JUST) Fellowship 2026. This unique four-month fellowship programme is designed to support field-based research, storytelling, and documentation around ecological, economic, and social transitions taking place across India. The JUST Fellowship 2026 offers selected fellows the […]

The post JUST Fellowship 2026 in India: Fully Funded Field-Based Fellowship on Just and Sustainable Transitions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Centre for Financial Accountability (CFA), in collaboration with Samata, has officially launched applications for the Just and Sustainable Transitions (JUST) Fellowship 2026. This unique four-month fellowship programme is designed to support field-based research, storytelling, and documentation around ecological, economic, and social transitions taking place across India.

The JUST Fellowship 2026 offers selected fellows the opportunity to engage directly with communities experiencing the impacts of mining, industrialization, climate transitions, ecological degradation, and shifting livelihoods. Through grounded research and community engagement, fellows will contribute to shaping conversations around equitable, democratic, and ecologically sustainable futures.

The fellowship particularly focuses on understanding how communities across India are navigating the realities of energy transitions, resource extraction, environmental degradation, labour transitions, governance challenges, and alternative development pathways.

About the JUST Fellowship 2026

The Just and Sustainable Transitions (JUST) Fellowship is a field-based initiative rooted in the idea that “just transition” goes far beyond simply replacing fossil fuels with renewable energy.

The fellowship explores broader structural and societal questions including:

  • Livelihoods and labour transitions
  • Land rights and governance
  • Ecological sustainability
  • Community participation
  • Public finance and welfare systems
  • Resource governance
  • Environmental justice
  • Decentralised economies
  • Community-led alternatives

Across India, communities are facing rapid transformations driven by:

  • Mining projects
  • Industrial expansion
  • Infrastructure development
  • Agricultural transitions
  • Conservation projects
  • Energy transition corridors

While many of these changes bring economic shifts, they also often result in:

  • Displacement
  • Ecological destruction
  • Loss of livelihoods
  • Increasing inequalities
  • Social and environmental conflicts

At the same time, many grassroots organisations, local communities, and collectives are creating innovative alternatives rooted in ecological restoration, democratic governance, and sustainable livelihoods.

The JUST Fellowship aims to document these realities through immersive field-based work and accessible storytelling.

Fellowship Focus Areas

The fellowship encourages applications exploring a wide range of transition-related themes, including but not limited to:

1. Mining and Resource Extraction Regions

Research may focus on communities affected by mining operations, extraction industries, and resource conflicts.

2. Energy Transition Corridors

Applicants may explore how renewable energy projects, energy infrastructure, and decarbonization efforts are impacting local populations.

3. Industrial and Emerging Industrial Zones

Projects may investigate industrial expansion, labour transitions, environmental impacts, and governance structures.

4. Agriculture and Agroecological Transitions

The fellowship supports work related to sustainable agriculture, agroecology, food systems, and rural livelihoods.

5. Ecological Restoration and Conservation Landscapes

Fellows may document community-led conservation efforts, restoration projects, and biodiversity initiatives.

6. Transport, Infrastructure, and Urban Transitions

Research can examine the social and environmental consequences of urbanisation and infrastructure expansion.

7. Post-Extractive Futures and Community Governance

Applicants are encouraged to explore alternative models of development and community-led governance systems.

8. Public Finance and Welfare Systems

Projects may analyse financial mechanisms, public spending, institutional systems, and welfare approaches connected to transition processes.

Fellowship Duration and Benefits

Selected fellows will receive:

Fellowship Duration

  • Four-month fellowship programme
  • Fellowship period: July to October 2026

Financial Support

  • Monthly stipend of Rs. 40,000
  • Travel support of up to Rs. 10,000 (where required)

Mentorship Support

Participants may receive mentorship support based on:

  • Project needs
  • Availability of thematic mentors
  • Area of specialization

The mentorship component is intended to strengthen fellows’ research, documentation, and storytelling capacities.

Who Can Apply?

The JUST Fellowship 2026 is open to Indian citizens with experience or demonstrated interest in:

  • Livelihoods
  • Labour rights
  • Environment
  • Ecology
  • Governance
  • Public policy
  • Media
  • Community work
  • Social justice
  • Development studies

The organisers particularly encourage applications from:

  • Researchers
  • Journalists
  • Independent writers
  • Practitioners
  • Grassroots workers
  • Community organisers

Applicants should ideally have:

  • 2–3 years of relevant experience
  • Interest in field-based research
  • Strong documentation and writing abilities
  • Commitment to ecological and social justice issues

Fellowship Outputs

Selected fellows will be expected to produce:

1. Long-Form Research Article

  • One in-depth article between 6,000–8,000 words

2. Public-Facing Article or Op-Ed

  • One concise article between 800–1,200 words
  • Adapted for public engagement and wider dissemination

The long-form outputs from all fellows will be compiled into a collective publication by the organisers.

Why the JUST Fellowship Matters

The JUST Fellowship stands out because it bridges multiple fields rather than fitting solely into journalism, academia, or policy research.

The programme creates a space where:

  • Field realities meet public discourse
  • Research connects with storytelling
  • Community experiences shape policy conversations
  • Ecological justice becomes central to transition debates

It also contributes to growing conversations around:

  • Climate justice
  • Democratic governance
  • Sustainable development
  • Equitable transitions
  • Community-led resilience
  • Public accountability

Application Deadline

Applications for the JUST Fellowship 2026 close on:

14 June 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare their applications early.

How to Apply

Applicants can access the fellowship concept notes in:

  • English
  • Hindi

Apply Here 

For more information about this fellowship visit here

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY on here

The post JUST Fellowship 2026 in India: Fully Funded Field-Based Fellowship on Just and Sustainable Transitions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026: Global Innovation Challenge in Switzerland for Future Entrepreneurs and Problem Solvers
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026 is now open for ambitious innovators, entrepreneurs, students, researchers, and creative thinkers from around the world who are ready to tackle real-world problems in one of Europe’s most immersive innovation experiences. Hosted by the ETH Entrepreneur Club in Switzerland, the InCube Challenge is a unique five-day innovation competition […]

The post ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026: Global Innovation Challenge in Switzerland for Future Entrepreneurs and Problem Solvers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026 is now open for ambitious innovators, entrepreneurs, students, researchers, and creative thinkers from around the world who are ready to tackle real-world problems in one of Europe’s most immersive innovation experiences.

Hosted by the ETH Entrepreneur Club in Switzerland, the InCube Challenge is a unique five-day innovation competition where selected participants live and work together inside a transparent glass cube while collaborating to solve pressing challenges presented by major global companies and institutions.

Taking place from 26–30 October 2026, this highly competitive program combines entrepreneurship, innovation, teamwork, mentorship, rapid prototyping, networking, leadership development, and live pitching into one unforgettable experience.

Unlike traditional hackathons or startup competitions, the InCube Challenge pushes participants beyond their comfort zones by immersing them in a high-intensity environment where creativity, collaboration, and problem-solving happen in real time under public visibility.

Applications for the ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026 are open from 1 May to 31 May 2026.

What is the ETH InCube Challenge?

The InCube Challenge is an immersive innovation competition organized by the ETH Entrepreneur Club, designed to bring together talented individuals from diverse backgrounds to solve practical industry and societal challenges.

Participants are grouped into multidisciplinary teams and placed inside specially designed glass cubes for five days. During this period, teams collaborate intensively to:

  • Analyze complex real-world problems
  • Develop innovative solutions
  • Prototype ideas rapidly
  • Receive mentorship from experts and industry leaders
  • Prepare final pitches for judges and audiences

The challenge culminates in a live finale where teams present their solutions before executives, innovation leaders, entrepreneurs, judges, and hundreds of students.

The program emphasizes:

  • Entrepreneurship
  • Sustainability
  • Innovation
  • Human-centered problem-solving
  • Technology-driven impact
  • Team collaboration
  • Leadership under pressure

Why the InCube Challenge Stands Out

The ETH InCube Challenge has become one of Europe’s most exciting entrepreneurship and innovation experiences because of its unconventional format and strong industry partnerships.

Participants do not simply attend workshops or seminars. Instead, they become fully immersed in a live innovation ecosystem where they must collaborate, think critically, and build practical solutions in real time.

The challenge allows participants to:

  1. Work directly on real-world industry problems
  2. Collaborate with talented peers from around the world
  3. Receive mentorship from experienced professionals
  4. Gain exposure to multinational companies
  5. Build entrepreneurial and leadership skills
  6. Expand professional networks
  7. Develop creative confidence
  8. Experience innovation under pressure
  9. Present solutions to high-level audiences
  10. Explore Switzerland’s innovation ecosystem

The Complete InCube Journey

The InCube experience is structured into several stages designed to prepare participants for the final innovation challenge.

1. PrepCube – Virtual Kickoff

The journey begins with a virtual onboarding phase where participants:

  • Meet fellow innovators
  • Learn about the challenge themes
  • Understand expectations and workflows
  • Begin early collaboration in immersive online environments

2. OutCube – Swiss Alps Retreat

Before entering the glass cube, participants travel to the Swiss Alps for a team-building and preparation retreat.

This stage helps participants:

  • Build team chemistry
  • Strengthen collaboration
  • Prepare mentally for the challenge
  • Develop early concepts and approaches
  • Experience Switzerland’s unique environment

The OutCube phase is scheduled for 20–24 October 2026.

3. InCube – The Main Challenge

The central part of the experience takes place from 26–30 October 2026.

Participants:

  • Live inside the cube
  • Collaborate intensively with teammates
  • Attend mentoring sessions
  • Conduct brainstorming and prototyping
  • Develop innovative solutions
  • Work under tight timelines

The challenge environment is designed to simulate real startup and innovation pressures.

4. Finale – Pitch and Win

At the end of the challenge, teams pitch their solutions before:

  • Industry leaders
  • Corporate executives
  • Innovation experts
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Students
  • Judges

The final presentations provide teams with visibility, recognition, networking opportunities, and the possibility of winning prizes.

Real Industry Challenges from Global Companies

One of the most impressive aspects of the InCube Challenge is its collaboration with major international corporations and institutions.

Previous challenge sponsors and partners include:

  • UBS
  • ABB
  • Roche
  • IQVIA
  • Siemens
  • AWS
  • Microsoft
  • Huawei
  • Red Bull
  • Swiss Post
  • Allianz
  • SIX
  • ETH Zurich
  • Axpo
  • Thermo Fisher Scientific
  • Hilti
  • PMI Science
  • BKW
  • Swiss Engineering

Participants work on real corporate and societal problems related to:

  • Artificial intelligence
  • Sustainability
  • Healthcare
  • Climate solutions
  • Energy transition
  • Smart buildings
  • Public engagement
  • Green energy
  • Digital innovation
  • Data analytics
  • Patient experience
  • Infrastructure
  • Technology adoption

Examples of Past Innovation Challenges

Thermo Fisher Scientific Challenge

Participants explored how data could help achieve net-zero sustainability goals within clinical trial services.

Siemens x AWS Challenge

Teams developed AI-driven solutions to improve smart building efficiency, sustainability, and occupant comfort.

Roche x IQVIA Challenge

Participants worked on improving patient and doctor experiences for lower-priority healthcare products.

Axpo Challenge

Teams explored ways to accelerate public adoption of sustainable technologies.

ABB Challenge

Participants focused on solving Switzerland’s energy storage and green energy demand challenges.

TransnetBW Challenge

Teams examined how young people could become more actively involved in the energy transition.

Benefits of Joining the ETH InCube Challenge 2026

Participants in the InCube Challenge gain access to numerous personal and professional benefits.

Key Benefits Include:

  • Exposure to global companies and innovation leaders
  • Hands-on experience solving real-world problems
  • Entrepreneurial and innovation training
  • Team collaboration experience
  • Mentorship from experts
  • Networking opportunities
  • Rapid prototyping experience
  • Leadership development
  • International exposure
  • Access to a global innovation community
  • Public speaking and pitching experience
  • Practical experience in design thinking and innovation methodologies

Who Should Apply?

The ETH InCube Challenge welcomes individuals from diverse academic and professional backgrounds who are passionate about innovation and problem-solving.

Ideal applicants include:

  • University students
  • Graduate students
  • Researchers
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Engineers
  • Designers
  • Innovators
  • Startup founders
  • Creatives
  • Scientists
  • Technology enthusiasts
  • Sustainability advocates

The program values:

  • Curiosity
  • Creativity
  • Teamwork
  • Leadership potential
  • Problem-solving ability
  • Adaptability
  • Entrepreneurial thinking

Important Dates for ETH InCube Challenge 2026

Application Timeline

  • Applications Open: 1 May 2026
  • Applications Close: 31 May 2026
  • Group Interviews: June 2026
  • Individual Interviews: June – July 2026
  • Final Decisions: August 2026
  • OutCube Retreat: 20–24 October 2026
  • InCube Challenge: 26–30 October 2026

Why Switzerland is the Perfect Innovation Hub

Switzerland is globally recognized for:

  • Innovation
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Research excellence
  • Sustainability leadership
  • Technology development

Hosting the challenge at ETH Zurich and across Swiss innovation locations gives participants exposure to one of the world’s strongest entrepreneurial ecosystems.

What Participants Can Expect

Participants joining the InCube Challenge can expect:

  • Intense collaboration
  • Real-world impact
  • Fast-paced innovation
  • Exposure to cutting-edge technologies
  • Career development opportunities
  • Global networking
  • Creative experimentation
  • High-energy teamwork
  • Mentorship-driven learning

The experience is designed not only to develop solutions, but also to transform participants into future innovators and leaders.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge application portal during the application period from 1 May to 31 May 2026.

Applicants should prepare for:

  • Group interviews
  • Individual interviews
  • Collaborative assessments
  • Innovation-focused discussions

Strong applications will typically demonstrate:

  • Passion for innovation
  • Team collaboration skills
  • Leadership potential
  • Curiosity and creativity
  • Interest in solving real-world challenges

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

The ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026 is more than an innovation competition. It is an immersive entrepreneurial experience designed to push participants beyond traditional learning environments and into real-world innovation.

By combining collaboration, mentorship, sustainability, entrepreneurship, and rapid problem-solving, the challenge creates a powerful environment where participants can develop transformative ideas while building meaningful global connections.

For aspiring innovators who want to gain practical experience, work with leading companies, and become part of an international entrepreneurial ecosystem, the InCube Challenge 2026 offers a truly unique opportunity.

For more information about this competition visit here 

To discover more opportunities on OFY, click here

The post ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026: Global Innovation Challenge in Switzerland for Future Entrepreneurs and Problem Solvers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: J-PAL Europe Policy and Research Associate 2026 in France: Fully Detailed Career Opportunity for Early-Career Researchers and Policy Professionals
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL) Europe has officially opened applications for the prestigious Policy and Research Associate position based in France for 2026. This exciting international opportunity is ideal for early-career professionals who are passionate about evidence-based policymaking, development economics, research, impact evaluation, and public policy. Hosted at the Paris School of […]

The post J-PAL Europe Policy and Research Associate 2026 in France: Fully Detailed Career Opportunity for Early-Career Researchers and Policy Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL) Europe has officially opened applications for the prestigious Policy and Research Associate position based in France for 2026. This exciting international opportunity is ideal for early-career professionals who are passionate about evidence-based policymaking, development economics, research, impact evaluation, and public policy.

Hosted at the Paris School of Economics, this position offers candidates a unique chance to work at the intersection of research, policy development, training, and international collaboration while contributing to global poverty reduction efforts through rigorous scientific evidence.

Applicants interested in policy research jobs in Europe, development economics careers, impact evaluation positions, and international public policy opportunities should pay close attention to this highly competitive role.

About J-PAL Europe

The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab, widely known as J-PAL, is a globally respected research center dedicated to reducing poverty through evidence-informed policymaking. J-PAL works with a network of more than 1,100 affiliated professors at universities worldwide and is recognized internationally for advancing randomized impact evaluations and rigorous policy research.

J-PAL Europe serves as one of J-PAL’s seven regional offices and focuses on generating and utilizing evidence to shape social programs and policies across Europe and beyond.

This particular role is connected to the “Development Methodologies Chair,” established jointly by:

  • The French Development Agency (AFD)
  • University Paris Sciences et Lettres (PSL)

The chair is led by Nobel Prize-winning economists and J-PAL co-founders:

  • Abhijit Banerjee
  • Esther Duflo

The initiative seeks to strengthen research in development economics and improve policymaking through scientific evidence and innovative methodologies.

Position Overview

Position Title

Policy and Research Associate – J-PAL Europe

Organization

J-PAL Europe

Location

France (based at the Paris School of Economics)

Earliest Start Date

1 August 2026

Latest Start Date

1 September 2026

This role provides hands-on professional experience for individuals interested in:

  • Development economics
  • Public policy
  • Humanitarian policy
  • Impact evaluations
  • Research communication
  • Evidence synthesis
  • Policy outreach
  • International development
  • Research capacity building

The selected candidate will support the creation of new research opportunities, strengthen partnerships, coordinate policy outreach, and contribute to training activities.

Why This Opportunity Is Important

This is not just another research assistant position. The J-PAL Europe Policy and Research Associate role provides direct exposure to:

  • International policymakers
  • Leading economists
  • Evidence-based policy initiatives
  • Research dissemination strategies
  • High-level development institutions
  • Policy conferences and workshops
  • Impact evaluation methodologies

The position is particularly valuable for candidates planning to pursue:

  • A PhD in economics or public policy
  • Careers in international organizations
  • Research leadership roles
  • Policy advisory careers
  • Development consulting
  • Humanitarian policy careers

Main Responsibilities of the Role

The selected candidate will contribute across several important areas.

1. Policy Development and Outreach

The associate will support evidence dissemination and policy engagement through:

  • Building relationships with:
    • Governments
    • International organizations
    • NGOs
    • Private-sector institutions
  • Identifying opportunities for rigorous evaluations
  • Preparing policy communication materials such as:
    • Policy briefs
    • Evaluation summaries
    • Presentations
    • Research insights
  • Translating complex academic findings into accessible formats for policymakers
  • Supporting new partnerships that encourage evidence-based policymaking
  • Writing:
    • Blog articles
    • Opinion editorials
    • Public communication materials
  • Coordinating:
    • Conferences
    • Workshops
    • Dissemination events
    • Panels and stakeholder engagements
  • Supporting outreach to:
    • Refugee-led organizations
    • Policy stakeholders
    • Development actors

2. Research Project Development

The associate will also help identify and support new impact evaluations by:

  • Identifying promising research opportunities
  • Engaging implementing partners
  • Exploring evaluation strategies
  • Supporting proposal development
  • Coordinating with J-PAL researchers
  • Assisting fundraising efforts where needed

This aspect of the role gives candidates direct exposure to real-world research implementation and experimental policy evaluation.

3. Training and Capacity Building

The role includes contributions to training programs designed for:

  • Policymakers
  • Project leaders
  • Researchers
  • Users of evidence-based findings

Responsibilities include:

  • Supporting training sessions
  • Developing learning materials
  • Helping communicate evaluation methodologies to diverse audiences

Professional Development Benefits

J-PAL Europe offers extensive opportunities for professional growth.

Selected candidates will gain access to:

Global Research Networks

Candidates will interact with world-leading researchers and development professionals.

Advanced Research Training

Exposure to:

  • Randomized evaluations
  • Causal inference
  • Quantitative research methodologies
  • Evidence synthesis techniques

International Exposure

Potential travel opportunities for:

  • Conferences
  • Workshops
  • Policy engagements
  • Dissemination activities

Career Advancement

The role is especially beneficial for individuals preparing for:

  • PhD programs
  • Research leadership
  • International development careers
  • Public policy careers

Eligibility Requirements

Candidates must meet several academic and professional requirements.

Educational Qualifications

Applicants should hold a Master’s degree in:

  • Economics
  • Public policy
  • Or a closely related field

Coursework should ideally include:

  • Microeconomics
  • Econometrics
  • Causal inference

Candidates from other disciplines may still qualify if they demonstrate:

  • Strong quantitative research interest
  • Knowledge of randomized evaluations
  • Evidence-based policy engagement

Required Experience

Applicants should have:

  • At least one year of relevant professional experience

Relevant experience may include:

  • Research assistantships
  • Research implementation
  • Policy research
  • Impact evaluations
  • Research capacity-building projects

Preferred Technical Skills

Strong preference will be given to candidates with experience in:

  • Randomized evaluations
  • Impact evaluation methods
  • Quantitative research
  • Evidence synthesis
  • Policy communication

Communication Skills

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Excellent English writing skills
  • Strong oral communication abilities
  • Ability to communicate with diverse stakeholders

Language Requirements

Required

  • Fluency in English

Preferred

  • French language skills
  • Other European languages

Additional Competencies

Ideal applicants should possess:

  • Excellent organizational skills
  • Strong time management
  • Attention to detail
  • Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously
  • Cultural sensitivity
  • Multicultural teamwork experience
  • International collaboration skills

Interest or experience in the humanitarian sector is considered an added advantage.

Travel Requirements

Candidates should be willing and able to travel internationally for project-related activities and events.

Application Process

Interested applicants must complete the J-PAL/IPA common application.

Applicants should:

  1. Register on the J-PAL application system
  2. Select the “Policy” job category
  3. Choose:
    “Research and Policy Associate – J-PAL Europe”

Apply Here 

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit:

  • CV/Resume
  • Cover letter
  • Reference contacts

The cover letter should explain:

  • Relevant experience
  • Qualifications
  • Motivation for the role
  • Authorization to work in the European Union

Incomplete applications will not be considered.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline:

31 May 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for interviews.

Why Candidates Should Apply Early

Competitive international research opportunities often receive large application volumes. Applying early may help candidates avoid technical delays and demonstrate strong interest in the position.

Career Opportunities After This Role

This opportunity can open doors to careers in:

  • International development
  • Academic research
  • Public policy consulting
  • Humanitarian organizations
  • Government advisory work
  • International NGOs
  • Multilateral institutions
  • Economic policy research

Many previous J-PAL staff members have gone on to:

  • Top PhD programs
  • International organizations
  • Research institutes
  • Global policy institutions

Final Thoughts

The J-PAL Europe Policy and Research Associate position in France represents one of the strongest international opportunities available for young professionals interested in research-driven policymaking and development economics.

Through direct collaboration with globally recognized researchers and policymakers, candidates will gain exceptional exposure to evidence-based policy work and international development research.

For aspiring economists, public policy professionals, researchers, and development practitioners, this role offers both meaningful impact and long-term career growth.

For more information on this job visit   here

Discover more job opportunities on OFY on here

The post J-PAL Europe Policy and Research Associate 2026 in France: Fully Detailed Career Opportunity for Early-Career Researchers and Policy Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNODC Global Youth Campaign 2026: Share Your Story to Prevent Youth Violence and Crime Worldwide
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) has launched an inspiring global youth campaign titled “Your Story Can Change the Path”, inviting young people from around the world to use creative expression to address youth violence, crime prevention, healing, belonging, and hope. This international initiative provides a powerful platform for young voices to […]

The post UNODC Global Youth Campaign 2026: Share Your Story to Prevent Youth Violence and Crime Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) has launched an inspiring global youth campaign titled “Your Story Can Change the Path”, inviting young people from around the world to use creative expression to address youth violence, crime prevention, healing, belonging, and hope.

This international initiative provides a powerful platform for young voices to contribute to global conversations on safer communities, rehabilitation, prevention, inclusion, and positive social change. Young people aged 18 to 29 are encouraged to share their experiences, perspectives, and creative work to help shape more effective and youth-centered approaches to crime prevention.

For young creatives, storytellers, artists, photographers, poets, musicians, activists, and advocates who are passionate about social impact, this is a unique opportunity to be featured in a United Nations-led global campaign.

About the UNODC Global Youth Campaign

The United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime recognizes that youth violence and crime affect communities across every region of the world. However, young people are often discussed in policymaking spaces without being given opportunities to speak for themselves.

This campaign aims to change that reality by creating a global platform where youth can openly share:

  • Their lived experiences
  • Their struggles
  • Their ideas
  • Their hopes
  • Their pathways toward prevention and healing

The campaign focuses on the understanding that building safe and just societies goes beyond law enforcement. Sustainable crime prevention also requires:

  • Addressing root causes
  • Strengthening protective factors
  • Supporting rehabilitation and reintegration
  • Expanding opportunities for young people
  • Promoting inclusion and belonging

By centering youth perspectives, the campaign seeks to improve how societies understand violence, prevention, resilience, and recovery.

What Makes This Opportunity Important?

This initiative is more than just a creative submission campaign. It is a global advocacy and awareness movement designed to:

  • Amplify youth voices
  • Challenge stereotypes
  • Encourage dialogue
  • Promote understanding
  • Influence prevention approaches
  • Support safer communities worldwide

The campaign particularly welcomes contributions from young people who have:

  • Experienced violence
  • Witnessed crime
  • Been impacted by violence
  • Been in conflict with the law
  • Experienced marginalization or exclusion

UNODC aims to ensure that prevention strategies are informed by those closest to these realities.

Who Can Apply?

The opportunity is open to:

  • Young people aged 18 to 29
  • Applicants from all countries and regions worldwide

The campaign especially encourages participation from:

  • Vulnerable youth
  • Youth advocates
  • Artists and storytellers
  • Young people affected by violence or crime
  • Individuals with lived experience related to justice systems
  • Community leaders
  • Social impact creatives

What Can Participants Submit?

Participants may use various forms of creative expression to tell their stories and perspectives.

Eligible submission formats include:

Creative Categories

  • Poetry
  • Personal stories
  • Drawings
  • Photography
  • Videos
  • Music
  • Mixed-media creations

UNODC emphasizes that if the work communicates a meaningful story or perspective related to youth, violence, prevention, healing, or hope, it belongs in the campaign.

Why Young People Should Participate

Participating in this campaign offers young people an opportunity to:

  • Share their voice globally
  • Inspire positive social change
  • Promote understanding and empathy
  • Contribute to international advocacy efforts
  • Challenge harmful stereotypes about youth
  • Support youth-centered prevention policies

Most importantly, participants become part of a larger movement focused on creating safer and more inclusive societies.

Global Recognition Opportunities

Selected submissions may be featured in major international platforms, including:

1. A Digital Youth Zine

Outstanding contributions may appear in a global digital youth publication showcasing stories and creative works from around the world.

2. UNTOC Youth Art Exhibition in Vienna

Selected works may be showcased at a youth art exhibition during UNTOC in Vienna in October 2026.

3. Global UNODC Advocacy Campaign

Creative works may also become part of an international advocacy campaign on youth crime prevention and violence reduction.

This provides participants with international visibility and the chance to contribute to important global discussions.

Themes the Campaign Encourages

Participants may explore themes such as:

  • Violence prevention
  • Rehabilitation
  • Reintegration
  • Belonging
  • Identity
  • Hope
  • Community healing
  • Youth empowerment
  • Social justice
  • Inclusion
  • Resilience
  • Safe communities
  • Opportunity creation
  • Overcoming stigma
  • Peacebuilding

Why Storytelling Matters in Crime Prevention

UNODC highlights that crime prevention is not only about policing or punishment. Real prevention requires:

  • Listening to young people
  • Understanding social realities
  • Addressing inequality
  • Supporting communities
  • Building trust and opportunity

Creative storytelling helps humanize experiences and provides deeper understanding of the social factors contributing to violence and crime.

Submission Deadline

Deadline:

31 July 2026

Applicants are encouraged to prepare thoughtful and authentic submissions before the deadline.

How to Participate

Interested participants can submit their creative work through the official UNODC submission platform.

Applicants should review:

  • Terms and Conditions
  • Participants Brief
  • Submission Guidelines

before submitting their work.

APPLY HERE 

Benefits of Participating

Participants may gain:

  • International exposure
  • Recognition by the United Nations
  • Advocacy opportunities
  • Exhibition opportunities
  • Networking visibility
  • Portfolio enhancement
  • Contribution to global social impact efforts

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

Unlike traditional competitions, this campaign focuses on:

  • Authentic lived experiences
  • Social impact
  • Youth empowerment
  • Creative advocacy
  • Community healing
  • Prevention-centered dialogue

It creates space for youth voices to directly influence conversations about safety, justice, and inclusion.

Final Thoughts

The UNODC “Your Story Can Change the Path” campaign is a powerful opportunity for young people to use creativity as a tool for advocacy, healing, and social transformation.

Through storytelling, art, music, photography, poetry, and other creative forms, participants can contribute to global efforts aimed at preventing violence, supporting rehabilitation, and building more inclusive societies.

Young people with important stories, perspectives, and experiences are encouraged to participate and help shape a future rooted in prevention, hope, and understanding.

For more information on this competition visit here 

Discover more opportunities on OFY on here 

The post UNODC Global Youth Campaign 2026: Share Your Story to Prevent Youth Violence and Crime Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026 Applications Open: Global Recognition for Sustainable Innovation and Impact
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) has officially opened applications for the prestigious ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026, inviting visionary companies, start-ups, and organizations from around the world to showcase transformative solutions that are reshaping industries through sustainability, innovation, and measurable impact. With applications open until June 30, 2026, the awards aim to recognize […]

The post UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026 Applications Open: Global Recognition for Sustainable Innovation and Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) has officially opened applications for the prestigious ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026, inviting visionary companies, start-ups, and organizations from around the world to showcase transformative solutions that are reshaping industries through sustainability, innovation, and measurable impact.

With applications open until June 30, 2026, the awards aim to recognize groundbreaking initiatives that are driving climate action, promoting inclusive economic growth, strengthening sustainable supply chains, and accelerating industrial innovation aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

The awards provide an exceptional global platform for innovators seeking international recognition, strategic partnerships, investment visibility, and access to influential networks across UN Member States, policymakers, investors, sustainability leaders, and industry experts.

What Are the UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards?

The ONE World Sustainability Awards are a global initiative led by the United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) to celebrate organizations and innovators delivering scalable and impactful sustainability solutions across industries.

The awards spotlight enterprises that are creating meaningful environmental, social, and economic change while demonstrating strong governance, innovation, and long-term sustainability.

Finalists and winners gain visibility on a global stage and become part of a prestigious international sustainability network connected to governments, businesses, development organizations, and innovation ecosystems worldwide.

The 2026 awards ceremony will take place in Vienna, Austria, offering finalists a unique opportunity to present their work before an international audience of decision-makers and stakeholders.

Inspiring Example: Oceanpick Pvt Ltd

One of the standout innovators highlighted by UNIDO is Oceanpick Pvt Ltd from Sri Lanka, founded by Irfan Thassim.

Oceanpick is recognized as Sri Lanka’s first commercially viable open-sea fish farm and demonstrates how sustainable innovation can transform food systems and coastal economies.

The company’s sea-to-fork aquaculture model focuses on:

  • Sustainable offshore aquaculture
  • Ocean restoration
  • Climate governance
  • Decarbonization
  • Circular value chains
  • Traceability and certified production systems
  • Resilient coastal livelihoods
  • Community-driven environmental restoration

Oceanpick represents the kind of innovation the awards seek to celebrate — solutions that combine sustainability, responsibility, scalability, and measurable community impact.

Why Apply for the ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026?

Organizations selected for the awards gain access to numerous high-value opportunities, including:

1. High Credibility Through UN Recognition

The awards are backed by UNIDO, a specialized agency of the United Nations dedicated to sustainable industrial development. Being associated with a UN-recognized platform significantly enhances organizational credibility and international visibility.

2. Global Visibility and Networking Opportunities

Participants become part of a global sustainability platform spanning 173 UN Member States. Finalists and winners can connect with:

  • Investors
  • Policymakers
  • Development agencies
  • Industry leaders
  • Sustainability experts
  • International partners

3. Independent and Transparent Evaluation

Applications are reviewed by an independent jury composed of globally respected experts in sustainability, economics, development, climate policy, innovation, and social impact.

The selection process follows strict standards of:

  • Transparency
  • Impartiality
  • Credibility
  • ESG alignment
  • SDG-focused evaluation

4. International Exposure in Vienna, Austria

The awards ceremony in Vienna provides finalists with a prestigious platform to showcase their innovations before a global audience, opening doors for collaboration, funding, and international partnerships.

Award Categories

The ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026 feature several categories designed to recognize different forms of impactful innovation.

Sustainable Supply Chains

This category recognizes organizations transforming sourcing systems, reducing emissions, supporting ethical labor practices, and creating sustainable local value chains.

Ideal applicants include companies working on:

  • Supply chain sustainability
  • Carbon reduction initiatives
  • Ethical sourcing
  • Circular economy systems
  • Community value creation

Innovative Start-Ups

Designed for breakthrough technologies and scalable business models, this category celebrates start-ups that are redefining industrial systems and sustainable innovation.

This category is suitable for:

  • Climate-tech start-ups
  • Green manufacturing innovators
  • Clean energy companies
  • Sustainable technology ventures
  • Circular economy innovators

Women in Industry

This category recognizes organizations that prioritize women’s empowerment and inclusion across technical, managerial, entrepreneurial, and industrial sectors.

Eligible initiatives may include:

  • Women-led enterprises
  • Organizations promoting women in leadership
  • Technical training programs for women
  • Women-focused industrial innovation projects

Key Evaluation Pillars

Applications will be assessed based on four major pillars:

Sustainability

Applicants must demonstrate a long-term commitment to environmental, social, and economic responsibility.

Impact

Organizations should provide measurable evidence of positive impact on communities, industries, livelihoods, and ecosystems.

Innovation

The awards seek novel and disruptive approaches that challenge traditional industrial systems and create sustainable alternatives.

Scalability

Projects should show potential for expansion, replication, and long-term sustainability across regions or industries.

Distinguished 2026 Jury Panel

Applications will be reviewed by a high-level independent jury consisting of internationally recognized experts and leaders from academia, policy, sustainability, economics, and development sectors.

Notable jury members include:

  • Jayati Ghosh
  • Tshilidzi Marwala
  • Kailash Satyarthi
  • Naila Kabeer
  • Ndidi Okonkwo Nwuneli
  • Yvonne Aki-Sawyerr
  • Deliang Chen

Their expertise ensures that applications are reviewed through a globally credible and multidisciplinary sustainability lens.

Who Should Apply?

The awards are ideal for:

  • Start-ups
  • SMEs
  • Large corporations
  • Social enterprises
  • Women-led businesses
  • Sustainability innovators
  • Industrial technology companies
  • Climate-tech ventures
  • Circular economy organizations
  • Supply chain innovators

Organizations with market-ready and scalable sustainability solutions are strongly encouraged to apply.

Application Deadline

Applications for the UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026 are open until:

June 30, 2026

Interested applicants should review the eligibility requirements and submit their applications before the deadline.

Final Thoughts

The UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026 present a powerful opportunity for organizations and innovators working at the intersection of sustainability, industrial transformation, and global development.

Whether through climate innovation, ethical supply chains, women’s empowerment, or scalable industrial technologies, the awards celebrate solutions capable of driving long-term positive change for people, industries, and the planet.

For companies seeking global recognition, international partnerships, and meaningful impact, the ONE World Sustainability Awards offer one of the most credible and influential sustainability platforms in the world.

Learn more and apply

Interested applicants can apply through the UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards application portal HERE.

Click HERE to learn more about the UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026.

Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

The post UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026 Applications Open: Global Recognition for Sustainable Innovation and Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: develoPPP Ventures 2026 Funding Programme for Start-ups in Africa | Catalytic Growth Funding for Innovative Businesses in Ghana, Kenya, Rwanda, South Africa, and Tanzania
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
African start-ups are increasingly becoming powerful drivers of innovation, job creation, economic transformation, and sustainable development across the continent. However, one of the biggest challenges facing early-stage and growth-stage businesses remains access to catalytic financing that can help them scale their impact and expand operations sustainably. To address this challenge, the develoPPP Ventures Funding Programme […]

The post develoPPP Ventures 2026 Funding Programme for Start-ups in Africa | Catalytic Growth Funding for Innovative Businesses in Ghana, Kenya, Rwanda, South Africa, and Tanzania appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
African start-ups are increasingly becoming powerful drivers of innovation, job creation, economic transformation, and sustainable development across the continent. However, one of the biggest challenges facing early-stage and growth-stage businesses remains access to catalytic financing that can help them scale their impact and expand operations sustainably.

To address this challenge, the develoPPP Ventures Funding Programme 2026 is offering an exciting opportunity for innovative start-ups operating in selected African countries to access growth financing and strategic support. The programme is designed to support businesses with scalable and development-oriented business models that contribute to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

The current call for applications is open from May 15 to June 30, 2026, and targets start-ups operating in Ghana, Kenya, Rwanda, South Africa, and Tanzania.

Implemented on behalf of the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), the programme is supported by implementing partners including DEG Impulse gGmbH and Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH.

Start-ups with innovative and scalable business models that create measurable development impact are strongly encouraged to apply.

 

What is develoPPP Ventures?

develoPPP Ventures is a funding initiative that supports start-ups in developing and emerging markets by providing catalytic funding for growth investments. The programme aims to strengthen entrepreneurial ecosystems and encourage private-sector solutions that address social, environmental, and economic challenges.

The programme specifically targets innovative start-ups that:

  • Already demonstrate proof of concept through initial revenues
  • Have strong potential for scalability
  • Contribute positively to sustainable development
  • Create measurable development impact
  • Operate with innovative business solutions

Unlike traditional grants, develoPPP Ventures focuses on businesses that combine commercial viability with development relevance.

 

Objectives of the Programme

The programme seeks to:

  1. Support innovative start-ups in emerging markets
  2. Encourage sustainable and inclusive economic growth
  3. Promote business models aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)
  4. Increase employment and entrepreneurship opportunities
  5. Strengthen local economies through impactful innovation
  6. Enable scalable businesses to attract further investment

By supporting high-potential start-ups, the programme helps businesses move from early growth stages toward long-term sustainability and expansion.

 

Current Target Countries for the 2026 Call

The ongoing develoPPP Ventures application cycle specifically targets start-ups operating in the following African countries:

  • Ghana
  • Kenya
  • Rwanda
  • South Africa
  • Tanzania

The programme provides local support teams to guide applicants during the application and selection process.

 

Funding Benefits for Selected Start-ups

Selected start-ups under the develoPPP Ventures programme may benefit from:

  • Catalytic co-financed growth investment
  • Financial support for scaling operations
  • Increased business visibility and credibility
  • Access to development-focused investment ecosystems
  • Support from international development partners
  • Opportunity to strengthen sustainable business impact
  • Enhanced potential for attracting additional investors and partners

The programme is particularly valuable for start-ups seeking strategic growth support while contributing to sustainable development outcomes.

 

Participation Requirements

To qualify for develoPPP Ventures 2026, applicants must meet the following participation requirements:

1. Company Registration Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Already be registered in the target country
    OR
  • Plan to register in the target country before receiving the co-financed investment

 

2. Innovative Business Model

The start-up must pursue an innovative business model and already generate initial revenues.

This means applicants should demonstrate:

  • Proof of concept
  • Existing customer traction
  • Market validation
  • Revenue-generating activities

 

3. Development Relevance and Scalability

Applicants must show that their business model:

  • Has strong development relevance
  • Is scalable
  • Contributes to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)

The programme prioritizes businesses capable of creating broad social or economic impact while maintaining commercial sustainability.

 

What Makes a Business Model Eligible?

The programme considers several important factors when evaluating start-ups.

Development Relevance

A business model is considered development-relevant when it:

  • Creates jobs
  • Improves livelihoods
  • Enhances sustainability
  • Solves social or environmental challenges
  • Supports inclusive economic growth

 

Innovation

The programme seeks businesses with innovative approaches, products, technologies, or services that address existing market challenges differently and effectively.

 

Scalability

Eligible businesses should demonstrate potential for expansion, replication, and long-term growth within or beyond their current markets.

 

Application Period

The current application window for develoPPP Ventures 2026 is:

May 15 to June 30, 2026

Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered.

 

How to Apply
